methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $8.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $7.0B.

data.narrative:
Wayve is a Series D artificial intelligence and software company founded by Amar Shah and Alex Kendall, currently valued at $8.6B post-money on $2.8B raised to date. The relevant peer universe comprises one public comparable (Palantir) and eight private peers across the AI & Software sector, including Cohere, Hugging Face, Hive AI, and Labelbox.

Two valuation methods anchor the analysis. Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yielding $12.9B. The peer-median approach references four same-stage, same-sector comparables with valuations ranging from $1.0B (Labelbox) to $7.0B (Cohere). This peer set exhibits a median valuation of $3.2B, substantially below Wayve's current $8.6B. These methodologies produce divergent signals: stage-progression implies upside to $12.9B, while peer-median suggests downside to $3.2B.

The triangulated range spans $2.8B (low) to $14.8B (high) with a median of $8.1B across both methods. Wayve's current $8.6B valuation sits near the analytical midpoint, suggesting fair value under this framework. The wide dispersion—with peer comps clustering below $5B while stage-progression implies $12.9B—reflects fundamental uncertainty around whether recent AI software funding dynamics support premium multiples relative to historical peers. Revenue, growth trajectory, and path to profitability were unavailable and represent critical missing inputs for refined valuation.
